turns.content: Hey, I'm trying to optimize my Rust app's Docker builds with cargo-chef. But every time I run docker build, it takes forever! How can I avoid that?

Ah, totally get you — Cargo + Docker caching is tricky sometimes. Imagine your Dockerfile as a recipe book; if every page is torn out and rewritten from scratch each time, it'd be slow. cargo-chef helps by pre-generating the optimal build layer order, just like organizing recipes to minimize flipping pages. Have you tried `cargo chef plan`?
Yeah, I did that, but then when I run `docker build`, it still seems to rebuild everything.

Good point. One common beginner mistake is forgetting to use the planner output in your Dockerfile. Think of cargo-chef as a chef who tells you which ingredients go first; if you forget to follow that order, it’s like mixing spices out of sequence — everything gets churned again. So make sure your Dockerfile starts with `COPY .cargo /opt/cargo` and then the rest in the planner's suggested order.
